revision.comment: 新しいページ：田ノ口古墳（高知県黒潮町にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 田ノ口古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
残存石室 |別名 = 田の口古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]下田ノ口宇野川史跡説明板。 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 0|緯度秒 = 57.28 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 32.58 |ISO = JP-39 |形状 = 不明（[[円墳]]か） |規模 = |築造年代 = [[古墳時代]]後期 |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = メノウ製勾玉・[[須恵器]] |史跡指定 = 高知県指定史跡「田ノ口古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kochi |ラベル = 田ノ口古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''田ノ口古墳'''（たのくちこふん、田の口古墳）は、[[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]下田の口にある[[古墳]]。高知県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 高知県西部、蠣瀬川左岸の小山丘中腹に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）の開墾の際に発見されたが、盛土・石室が破壊を受けているため全容は詳らかでない{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に円形ともいわれる。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方（蠣瀬川対岸の旗山神社の方角）に開口する{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。ただし羨道は破壊されており、現在は玄室の一部のみを遺存する{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。玄室は奥行1.63メートル・中央幅1.25メートル・入口幅1.38メートルを測る{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。出土品としてはメノウ製勾玉・[[須恵器]]があったという{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。以上より、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に高知県指定史跡に指定された。なお、その指定の際には幡多郡域で唯一の横穴式石室古墳になることが重要視されたが、その後に[[古津賀古墳]]（[[四万十市]]古津賀）などでも横穴式石室が確認されている。 File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|遠景
{{small|中央に石室。手前は[[国道56号]]、左端には田ノ口小学校が所在。}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|玄室内部 == 文化財 == === 高知県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 田ノ口古墳 - 1953年（昭和28年）1月29日指定[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳]（高知県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（黒潮町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳] - 高知県ホームページ * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 田ノ口古墳] - 黒潮町ホームページ 観光情報 {{デフォルトソート:たのくちこふん}} [[Category:高知県の古墳]] [[Category:黒潮町]] [[Category:高知県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 田ノ口古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
残存石室 |別名 = 田の口古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]下田ノ口宇野川史跡説明板。 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 0|緯度秒 = 57.28 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 32.58 |ISO = JP-39 |形状 = 不明（[[円墳]]か） |規模 = |築造年代 = [[古墳時代]]後期 |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = メノウ製勾玉・[[須恵器]] |史跡指定 = 高知県指定史跡「田ノ口古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kochi |ラベル = 田ノ口古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''田ノ口古墳'''（たのくちこふん、田の口古墳）は、[[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]下田の口にある[[古墳]]。高知県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 高知県西部、蠣瀬川左岸の小山丘中腹に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）の開墾の際に発見されたが、盛土・石室が破壊を受けているため全容は詳らかでない{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に円形ともいわれる。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方（蠣瀬川対岸の旗山神社の方角）に開口する{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。ただし羨道は破壊されており、現在は玄室の一部のみを遺存する{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。玄室は奥行1.63メートル・中央幅1.25メートル・入口幅1.38メートルを測る{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。出土品としてはメノウ製勾玉・[[須恵器]]があったという{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。以上より、築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に高知県指定史跡に指定された。なお、その指定の際には幡多郡域で唯一の横穴式石室古墳になることが重要視されたが、その後に[[古津賀古墳]]（[[四万十市]]古津賀）などでも横穴式石室が確認されている。 File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|遠景
{{small|中央に石室。手前は[[国道56号]]、左端には田ノ口小学校が所在。}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|玄室内部 == 文化財 == === 高知県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 田ノ口古墳 - 1953年（昭和28年）1月29日指定[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳]（高知県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（黒潮町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳] - 高知県ホームページ * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 田ノ口古墳] - 黒潮町ホームページ 観光情報 {{デフォルトソート:たのくちこふん}} [[Category:高知県の古墳]] [[Category:黒潮町]] [[Category:高知県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Information [[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Information [[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Information [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[ja:[[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:[[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Information [[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[ja:[[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[ja:[[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:[[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Information [[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | shape = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view

It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view

Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Tumulus | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alias = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historical Site Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]other_designation Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus shape is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]other_designation Explanation Board. | latitude = 33.0158 | longitude = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) | size = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | burial_facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | deceased = | artifacts = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label = Tanokuchi Kofun | icon = | label_position = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. Artifacts found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of designation, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi
