revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kuroshio, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 田ノ口古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
残存石室 |別名 = 田の口古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]下田ノ口宇野川史跡説明板。 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 0|緯度秒 = 57.28 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 32.58 |ISO = JP-39 |形状 = 不明（[[円墳]]か） |規模 = |築造年代 = [[古墳時代]]後期 |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = メノウ製勾玉・[[須恵器]] |史跡指定 = 高知県指定史跡「田ノ口古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kochi |ラベル = 田ノ口古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''田ノ口古墳'''（たのくちこふん、田の口古墳）は、[[高知県]][[幡多郡]][[黒潮町]]下田の口にある[[古墳]]。高知県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 高知県西部、蠣瀬川左岸の小山丘中腹に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）の開墾の際に発見されたが、盛土・石室が破壊を受けているため全容は詳らかでない{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に円形ともいわれる。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方（蠣瀬川対岸の旗山神社の方角）に開口する{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。ただし羨道は破壊されており、現在は玄室の一部のみを遺存する{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。玄室は奥行1.63メートル・中央幅1.25メートル・入口幅1.38メートルを測る{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。出土品としてはメノウ製勾玉・[[須恵器]]があったという{{Sfn|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。以上より、築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に高知県指定史跡に指定された。なお、その指定の際には幡多郡域で唯一の横穴式石室古墳になることが重要視されたが、その後に[[古津賀古墳]]（[[四万十市]]古津賀）などでも横穴式石室が確認されている。 File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|遠景
{{small|中央に石室。手前は[[国道56号]]、左端には田ノ口小学校が所在。}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|玄室内部 == 文化財 == === 高知県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 田ノ口古墳 - 1953年（昭和28年）1月29日指定[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳]（高知県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（黒潮町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳] - 高知県ホームページ * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 田ノ口古墳] - 黒潮町ホームページ 観光情報 {{デフォルトソート:たのくちこふん}} [[Category:高知県の古墳]] [[Category:黒潮町の歴史]] [[Category:高知県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[田ノ口古墳]] to [[Tanokuchi Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{small|中央に石室。手前は[[国道56号]]、左端には田ノ口小学校が所在。}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|玄室内部 == 文化財 == === 高知県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 田ノ口古墳 - 1953年（昭和28年）1月29日指定[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳]（高知県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（黒潮町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm 田ノ口古墳] - 高知県ホームページ * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 田ノ口古墳] - 黒潮町ホームページ 観光情報 {{デフォルトソート:たのくちこふん}} [[Category:高知県の古墳]] [[Category:黒潮町の歴史]] [[Category:高知県指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshio, Kōchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata District, Kōchi|qq=}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|ja|黒潮町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshio, Kōchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|ja|幡多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata District, Kōchi|qq=}}, [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/magatama|qq=}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi|ja|四万十市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanto, Kōchi|qq=}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:黒潮町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}}

Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = 33.0158 | coordinates = 132.9924 | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:黒潮町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}}

It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}}

Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tanokuchi Kofun]] [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}}

In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}}
