revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tanokuchi Kofun]][[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanokuchi Kofun | image = [[File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kuroshio, Kōchi|Kuroshio, Kōchi]], [[:en:Hata District, Kōchi|Hata District, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], suggesting a construction period in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|Shimanto, Kōchi]]. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|en|Kuroshio, Kōchi|WD=Q1351626|lt=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|en|Hata District, Kōchi|WD=Q1132241|lt=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|en|Kuroshio, Kōchi|WD=Q1351626|lt=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|en|Hata District, Kōchi|WD=Q1132241|lt=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|en|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|WD=Q166946|lt=Shimanto, Kōchi}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|en|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|WD=Q166946|lt=Shimanto, Kōchi|1=Shimanto, Kōchi (city)}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Remaining stone chamber | alternate_name = Tanokuchi Kofun | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|en|Kuroshio, Kōchi|WD=Q1351626|lt=Kuroshio, Kōchi|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|en|Hata District, Kōchi|WD=Q1132241|lt=Hata District, Kōchi|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}Historic Site Explanation Board. | coordinates = {{Coord|33.015911|N|132.992383|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown (possibly a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | Burial Facility = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | deceased = | burial goods = Agate magatama, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Tanokuchi Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = Tanokuchi Kofun | type = | map_dot_label = top }} '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' (田ノ口古墳, also known as 田の口古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kuroshio, Kōchi|en|Kuroshio, Kōchi|WD=Q1351626|lt=Kuroshio, Kōchi|1=Kuroshio, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Hata District, Kōchi|en|Hata District, Kōchi|WD=Q1132241|lt=Hata District, Kōchi|1=Hata District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is situated on the mid-slope of a small hill on the left bank of the Kakise River in western Kōchi Prefecture. It was discovered in 1907 during land clearing activities, but much of the mound and the stone chamber were destroyed, leaving the full details of the structure unclear. While the tumulus type is uncertain, some suggest it might have been circular. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest toward the direction of Hatayama Shrine across the Kakise River. The entrance passage has been destroyed, leaving only part of the burial chamber intact. The chamber measures 1.63 meters in depth, 1.25 meters in central width, and 1.38 meters in entrance width. burial goods found at the site include an agate {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, suggesting a construction period in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. At the time of Historic Site, it was considered the only yokoana-style kofun in the Hata District. However, similar chambers have since been discovered, including at the {{ill|Furutsuka Kofun|ja|古津賀古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furutsuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|en|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|WD=Q166946|lt=Shimanto, Kōchi|1=Shimanto, Kōchi (city)}}. File:Tanokuchi Kofun, enkei.jpg|Distant view
{{small|The stone chamber is visible in the center. In the foreground are National Route 56 and Tanokuchi Elementary School on the far left.}} File:Tanokuchi Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the burial chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tanokuchi Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (installed by Kuroshio Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=田の口古墳|title={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Kōchi Prefecture|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 40 Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|田の口古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tanokuchi Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-31.htm Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.town.kuroshio.lg.jp/pb/cont/kuronavi-asobu/4142 Tanokuchi Kofun] – Kuroshio Town Tourism Intypeation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|田ノ口古墳|version=92523860|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:田ノ口古墳]] {{Commons category|Tanokuchi Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522046}} [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kuroshio]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
